titleStmt.title: Letter from Delmege Trimble to Lady Clonbrock, 25 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Delmege Trimble

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Delmege Trimble, assistant editor the Royal Irish Fusiliers' gazette and a member of the Royal Irish Fusiliers Comforts Fund to Augusta Caroline Dillon (née Crofton), Lady Clonbrock. Lady Clonbrock was a member of the Irish Women's Association, set up to provide assistance to Irish regiments and Irish prisoners of war. Trimble writes enclosing a list of names of soldiers in the Connaught Rangers in a hospital in Sofia with sizes of boots and clothes to send to them.

msIdentifier.collection: Clonbrock Estate Papers, Collection List No 54; Ms. 35,796

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Royal Irish Fusiliers Comforts Fund
For Men In the Fighting Lines and Prisoners of War.
ARMAGH GUARDIAN OFFICE,
Armagh 25th April 1916
Delmege Trimble
Assistant Editor
"Faugh = a = Ballagh."
Regimental Gazette of the
Royal Irish Fusiliers. The Lady Clonbrock,
Clonbrock,
Ahascragh Madam, I am in receipt of your letter of 20 th inst. and
herewith enclose you list of Connaught Rangers
prisoners, ,at 5th Divisional Hospital, Sophia , with sizes
for clothes and boots, and note of wants most needed. The information, which came from an
Irish Fusilier N.C.O., also says there are 10 more
men in Sophia. I make no charge for helping another
Prisoner's Fund. Yours faithfully, Delmege Trimble
